data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T3 (Integration feasibility): **PASS (no findings).**

T3 is an overlay that applies only to external API/CLI/service/library integrations or asserted-but-unused capabilities. This ticket is documentation-only against rebar's OWN internal, already-used subsystems, so the rubric's ANTI-FP rule ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable") governs: not-applicable → PASS. No external auth/HTTPS surface is introduced.

Affirmation: I verified every named entity the plan promises to document actually exists in the current source (MATCH), so the "no drift" intent (AC line "verified against the current source") is feasible:
- signing.py: signing_key, key_fingerprint, parse_manifest, compute_signature, verify_record, head_sha, sign_manifest, verify_signature all present; verdict vocabulary certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned present; ALGORITHM/PAYLOAD_VERSION canonicalisation present.
- llm/runner.py: Runner protocol (run/preflight), RunRequest with mode/execution_mode/output_schema/extra_tools, get_runner all present.
- llm/workflow/executor.py: run_workflow present.
- llm/prompts.py: get_prompt, resolve_prompt present; front-matter/execution_mode contract present.
- llm/findings.py: validate_structured present.

No T3 findings to address.
